title: SniperBot Asset-Class Eligibility / Exclusion Decision Schema
description: Pure, deterministic, immutable, side-effect-free, non-authorizing, non-executing descriptive classification of externally supplied generic asset-class eligibility, exclusion, restriction, evidence, and optional contextual authority facts. ELIGIBLE means only continued governed Stage 2 evaluation and never permission, asset approval, downstream-gate completion, trading, transaction, execution, integration, or Stage 3 authority. The future evaluator owns the approved 12-step first-match precedence and mappings; this schema intentionally encodes no procedural conditionals and preserves governed conflicts and undefined combinations as structurally valid typed requests.

AssetClass.description: Exactly one explicitly supplied generic asset class. It is never inferred from an identifier, contract, token, broker, exchange, wallet, venue, market data, or metadata.

Outcome.description: Closed descriptive outcomes only. None grants EchoAuth permission, market access, asset approval, order or transaction authority, execution authority, or completion of downstream gates.

ReasonCode.description: Closed 15-value reason vocabulary. UNKNOWN, free text, aliases, hidden fallbacks, Deferral reasons, and lane-specific reasons are not approved.

RequiredAction.description: Full five-value repository vocabulary. FOUNDER_AUTHORITY_REQUIRED and RESET_REQUIRED are vocabulary-only and cannot be emitted by this subject. NONE means only that this classifier requires no additional action; it never means permission.

EmittableAction.description: The exact three RequiredAction values this subject may emit.

evidence_reference.description: Opaque non-empty caller-supplied evidence reference. It is not parsed, inferred, looked up, or interpreted.

AuthorityEvidence.description: Optional contextual EchoAuth evidence only. The future evaluator uses the founder-approved subprecedence: contradictory; AMBIGUOUS; INVALID; revoked; not current; not in scope. A fully valid object permits evaluation to continue to lower branches only. It grants no authority or eligibility and does not call, validate, repair, reinterpret, broaden, grant, replace, or bypass EchoAuth.

eligibility_reference.description: Opaque non-empty eligibility reference only; no individual asset, identifier, venue, account, market, or selection semantics are represented.

eligibility_evidence_contradictory.description: Only this supplied flag establishes eligibility-evidence contradiction.

asset_class_eligible.description: Externally supplied descriptive eligibility fact. It is not calculated, inferred, verified, modified, or treated as permission.

asset_class_excluded.description: Externally supplied descriptive exclusion fact. It is not calculated, verified, derived, modified, removed, overridden, narrowed, or broadened.

asset_class_restricted.description: Externally supplied descriptive restriction fact. It is not calculated, verified, derived, modified, removed, overridden, narrowed, or broadened.

authority_evidence.description: Optional by property omission only. null and an empty object are invalid; absence creates no authority, permission, failure branch, or inferred eligibility.

correlation_reference.description: Opaque non-empty caller reference; exact equality with decision.correlation_reference is evaluator-owned.

Request.description: Closed request of externally supplied generic asset-class facts only. Eligible/excluded, eligible/restricted, excluded/restricted, and the three approved undefined predicates remain structurally valid for future evaluator classification. The future evaluator owns the exact order: evidence contradiction; eligible/excluded; eligible/restricted; governed undefined combination; exclusion; restriction; supplied authority failure; evidence missing; stale; insufficient; external eligible; unresolved fallback. No Deferral context, lane confirmation, lane-specific result, individual asset, security, option, contract, token, coin, ticker, symbol, venue, exchange, wallet, account, broker, custody, blockchain, address, market-data, price, liquidity, signal, strategy, risk, sizing, simulation, replay, backtesting, order, transaction, routing, execution, integration, or runtime-wiring field is represented.

eligibility_reference.description: Must exactly preserve request.eligibility_reference; cross-object equality is evaluator-owned.

correlation_reference.description: Must exactly preserve request.correlation_reference; cross-object equality is evaluator-owned.

Decision.description: Closed descriptive output only. ELIGIBLE means continued governed Stage 2 evaluation, not permission or completion of downstream gates. The future evaluator owns exact mappings, precedence, reference preservation, immutability, and non-mutation. No other evaluator is invoked or orchestrated, and no selection, access, market data, strategy, risk, sizing, order, transaction, routing, persistence, runtime, integration, trading, or execution behavior is represented.
